title: Unhandled git rebase error crashes sync continue/abort flow
description: runContinueSync and runAbortSync invoke defaultRebaseContinue / defaultRebaseAbort without catching thrown errors. If git rebase --continue/--abort fails (unstaged conflicts, other git errors), the exception propagates uncaught and crashes the CLI, leaving sync-session.json in a conflicted/stale state and locking the CLI in an unrecoverable sync loop.
fix_direction: Wrap the rebase ops in try-catch in runContinueSync/runAbortSync, record failure/abort cleanup in the state machine/session file, and return a clean error diagnostic instead of crashing.

title: Non-atomic session writes and unhandled parse error crash the CLI
description: saveSession writes sync-session.json with writeFileSync directly (no write-tmp+rename, no fsync); interruption/power loss corrupts it. loadSession parses via JSON.parse(readFileSync(...)) with no try-catch, so any later command (fabric sync --continue) crashes with an unhandled SyntaxError, permanently breaking sync until the session file is manually deleted.
fix_direction: Use atomicWriteJson (with fsync) in saveSession, and wrap JSON.parse in loadSession in try-catch to handle corruption gracefully (warn + return null / reclaim new session).

title: TOCTOU race and lock theft in HTTP server serve-lock
description: acquireLock verifies lock existence with fs.existsSync(p) then fs.writeFileSync(p,...), a TOCTOU race. Two concurrently starting HTTP servers can both find it empty/stale and write, one silently overwriting the other -> double execution. On shutdown releaseLock checks state.pid===process.pid and unlinks; if a new process overtook the lock, the old process's release deletes the active lock, causing accidental release.
fix_direction: Acquire atomically with fs.writeFileSync(p, content, { flag: 'wx' }) / openSync(p,'wx') so only one process succeeds; throw ServeLockHeldError if it already exists.

title: Explicit retrieval IDs silently truncated by semantic top-k budget
description: In combined recall, an explicit ids list is intersected with planContext semantic candidates via effectiveIds.filter(id => candidateIds.includes(id)). Because planContext truncates candidates to top_k (~30-50), any explicitly requested ID ranking poorly semantically is excluded silently, and the caller gets an empty result with no error/diagnostic that their requested rules were truncated.
fix_direction: Let explicitly requested ids bypass planContext top-k truncation, or make planContext aware of explicit ids and prioritize them so they are never omitted by the cap.

title: Non-atomic store scaffolding and race condition in initStore
description: initStore checks existsSync(identityFile) then mkdirSync + writeFileSync(identityFile,...) — not atomic. Interruption/disk-full mid-way leaves a half-initialized corrupted directory. With no lock-based concurrency control, concurrent storeCreate processes can both pass the existence check and overwrite store.json simultaneously.
fix_direction: Wrap initialization in withFileLock (or a local lock file) for mutual exclusion and roll back created folders on write errors to avoid partial-write corruption.
